{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accounts Payable Officer",
  "description": "Brainshare Technologies &amp; Services Nigeria Limited is a Telecom services provider. We offer connectivity, managed services and other value added services through our fiber optic infrastructure.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts Payable Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Accounts Payable Specialist is responsible for providing reports on the company&#39;s financial exposures, coordinating with user departments on vendor payments, maintaining up-to-date records of vendor information and ensuring timely and accurate payments to vendors.


Main Duties


	Prepare and provide reports on the company&#39;s financial exposures, including accounts payable and accruals.
	Coordinate with user departments to ensure timely and accurate payment to vendors.
	Verify and process vendor invoices and payment requests.
	Process employees expense reports and reimbursement requests.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and procedures regarding vendor information management.
	Prepare and present monthly payroll, payslip, departmental budgets, payment vouchers for approval.
	Process vendor payments in a timely and accurate manner.
	Ensure that all payments are properly authorized and documented.
	Provide payment advice to vendors on a timely basis.
	Maintain up-to-date and accurate records of vendor information, including contact details and payment terms.
	Respond to vendor inquiries and resolve any payment-related issues.
	Maintain filing and organization of payment confirmation, invoices and documentation.
	Update and maintain vendor database in a timely manner.
	Generating payment schedule in an accurate and timely manner.
	Perform reconciliations including vendors/contractors statement reconciliations on a weekly basis.
	Investigate &amp; resolveVendors query on payments.
	Perform bank reconciliations and report.
	Report on Aged Payables plus other ad-hoc reporting where applicable.
	Support Accounts team with necessary audit requirement.
	Verifies Vendors accounts by reconciling monthly statements &amp; related transactions.
	Maintains accounting ledgers by verifying &amp; posting account transactions.
	Preparation and payment of Monthly Sales Commission.
	Any other Tasks assigned to you by the Management.


Personal Qualities &amp; Qualifications
Job Holder must possess the following:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting from reputable higher institution with a minimum of 3 years of Accounts Payable, Data Entry or related experience
	Good knowledge of financial software.
	Attention to detail &amp; thoroughness.
	Analytical mind &amp; ability to drive revenue collection at record time.
	Demonstrates sense of urgency &amp; tenacious at work.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to:&nbsp;careers@brainshare.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",

    "@type": "Organization",

Accounts Payable Officer Job at Brainshare Technologies & Services Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Brainshare Technologies & Services Nigeria Limited is a Telecom services provider. We offer connectivity, managed services and other value added services through our fiber optic infrastructure.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts Payable Officer

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Accounts Payable Specialist is responsible for providing reports on the company's financial exposures, coordinating with user departments on vendor payments, maintaining up-to-date records of vendor information and ensuring timely and accurate payments to vendors.


Main Duties


	Prepare and provide reports on the company's financial exposures, including accounts payable and accruals.
	Coordinate with user departments to ensure timely and accurate payment to vendors.
	Verify and process vendor invoices and payment requests.
	Process employees expense reports and reimbursement requests.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and procedures regarding vendor information management.
	Prepare and present monthly payroll, payslip, departmental budgets, payment vouchers for approval.
	Process vendor payments in a timely and accurate manner.
	Ensure that all payments are properly authorized and documented.
	Provide payment advice to vendors on a timely basis.
	Maintain up-to-date and accurate records of vendor information, including contact details and payment terms.
	Respond to vendor inquiries and resolve any payment-related issues.
	Maintain filing and organization of payment confirmation, invoices and documentation.
	Update and maintain vendor database in a timely manner.
	Generating payment schedule in an accurate and timely manner.
	Perform reconciliations including vendors/contractors statement reconciliations on a weekly basis.
	Investigate & resolveVendors query on payments.
	Perform bank reconciliations and report.
	Report on Aged Payables plus other ad-hoc reporting where applicable.
	Support Accounts team with necessary audit requirement.
	Verifies Vendors accounts by reconciling monthly statements & related transactions.
	Maintains accounting ledgers by verifying & posting account transactions.
	Preparation and payment of Monthly Sales Commission.
	Any other Tasks assigned to you by the Management.


Personal Qualities & Qualifications
Job Holder must possess the following:


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting from reputable higher institution with a minimum of 3 years of Accounts Payable, Data Entry or related experience
	Good knowledge of financial software.
	Attention to detail & thoroughness.
	Analytical mind & ability to drive revenue collection at record time.
	Demonstrates sense of urgency & tenacious at work.


Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

	Interest or experience in fertility/ART and HIFU is a plus

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruiting@nordicalagos.org&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Consultant Gynecologist Job at Nordica Fertility Centre




        
    

        
        Nordica Fertility Centre was established by, Dr. Abayomi Ajayi, in association with Nordica International, Denmark, to provide the latest and most advanced assisted reproductive services for the treatment of male and female infertility and related issues.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Consultant Gynecologist

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Requirements
Who We’re Looking For:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	FWACS / FMCOG (or equivalent)
	Licensed to practice in Nigeria
	Interest or experience in fertility/ART and HIFU is a plus
	Committed to high-quality, compassionate care.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Production Supervisor",
  "description": "Deen Consult is a dynamic professional services firm specializing in Recruitment, Training, and General Business Consultation. Established with a passion for helping organizations achieve sustainable growth, we serve as a trusted partner to businesses across diverse industries. Our mission is to bridge the gap between talent, knowledge, and performance, ensuring that our clients remain competitive in today&rsquo;s fast-changing business environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Production Supervisor

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	The Production Supervisor oversees daily manufacturing operations, ensuring that production targets are met efficiently, safely, and within quality standards.
	They act as the link between management and production staff, coordinating workflows, troubleshooting issues, and implementing process improvements to optimize productivity.


Responsibilities
Production Management:


	Plan, organize, and monitor production schedules to meet customer demand and deadlines.
	Allocate tasks and assign shifts to operators and technicians.
	Monitor workflow and ensure smooth, efficient production processes.
	Ensure machines, tools, and raw materials are available for uninterrupted operations.


Quality Assurance:


	Ensure products meet company and regulatory quality standards.
	Work closely with the Quality Control team to address defects and implement corrective measures.
	Maintain records of production quality checks and audits.


Staff Supervision and Training:


	Supervise production teams, providing guidance and motivation to meet performance standards.
	Conduct training sessions on safety, quality, and process improvements.
	Monitor employee performance and address performance issues.


Health, Safety &amp; Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with workplace health and safety regulations.
	Conduct regular safety drills and inspections.
	Investigate and report accidents or near-miss incidents promptly.


Reporting &amp; Documentation:


	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly production reports.
	Maintain accurate records of production volume, downtime, and workforce utilization.
	Report equipment breakdowns and coordinate maintenance needs.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Minimum of HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Food Science, Biochemistry, or Microbiology.
	3 - 6years of experience in manufacturing or production supervision (FMCG preferred).
	Strong understanding of production workflows, machinery, and quality standards.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills.
	Knowledge of production processes, machinery, and quality systems.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;deenconsult@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.",

Production Supervisor Job at Deen Consult




        
    

        
        Deen Consult is a dynamic professional services firm specializing in Recruitment, Training, and General Business Consultation. Established with a passion for helping organizations achieve sustainable growth, we serve as a trusted partner to businesses across diverse industries. Our mission is to bridge the gap between talent, knowledge, and performance, ensuring that our clients remain competitive in today’s fast-changing business environment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Production Supervisor

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Production Supervisor oversees daily manufacturing operations, ensuring that production targets are met efficiently, safely, and within quality standards.
	They act as the link between management and production staff, coordinating workflows, troubleshooting issues, and implementing process improvements to optimize productivity.


Responsibilities
Production Management:


	Plan, organize, and monitor production schedules to meet customer demand and deadlines.
	Allocate tasks and assign shifts to operators and technicians.
	Monitor workflow and ensure smooth, efficient production processes.
	Ensure machines, tools, and raw materials are available for uninterrupted operations.


Quality Assurance:


	Ensure products meet company and regulatory quality standards.
	Work closely with the Quality Control team to address defects and implement corrective measures.
	Maintain records of production quality checks and audits.


Staff Supervision and Training:


	Supervise production teams, providing guidance and motivation to meet performance standards.
	Conduct training sessions on safety, quality, and process improvements.
	Monitor employee performance and address performance issues.


Health, Safety & Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with workplace health and safety regulations.
	Conduct regular safety drills and inspections.
	Investigate and report accidents or near-miss incidents promptly.


Reporting & Documentation:


	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly production reports.
	Maintain accurate records of production volume, downtime, and workforce utilization.
	Report equipment breakdowns and coordinate maintenance needs.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Minimum of HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Food Science, Biochemistry, or Microbiology.
	3 - 6years of experience in manufacturing or production supervision (FMCG preferred).
	Strong understanding of production workflows, machinery, and quality standards.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills.
	Knowledge of production processes, machinery, and quality systems.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
